beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, check weather and work emails
beats.reasoning: Viktor starts his Monday at full energy; sunny weather lifts his mood. He's a meticulous planner, so morning email check and coffee ritual are essential before heading to the Tukwila site.

beats.activity: Drive south to Tukwila construction site
beats.reasoning: Light Rail is down 9am–3pm, but Viktor drives anyway; no impact on his commute route. Early departure ensures he's on-site before the crew arrives.

beats.activity: Manage construction crew, inspect framing, review schedule and budget
beats.reasoning: Four-hour work block; Viktor is hyper-focused and meticulous with spreadsheets, so he stays on-site managing the project. The 11am FIFA match is downtown and irrelevant to his workday—he's not a soccer fan and won't interrupt his site management.

beats.activity: Quick lunch at jobsite trailer or nearby deli
beats.reasoning: Viktor is efficient and stays on-site during lunch; no reason to leave the area. Keeps him close to work and saves travel time.

beats.activity: Continue site management, final inspections, prepare end-of-day report
beats.reasoning: Full afternoon work block; Viktor is a workaholic and stays focused on the project through mid-afternoon. Meticulous documentation of the day's progress is part of his routine.
Evening commute home; Light Rail maintenance is ending at 3pm, but Viktor continues his car routine. Drive time is slightly longer due to evening traffic but manageable.

beats.activity: Stop at QFC for fresh flour, sourdough starter supplies, and dinner groceries
beats.reasoning: Viktor values his sourdough hobby; the sunny Monday and good energy make this an ideal evening to restock supplies. Quick errand in his neighborhood on the way home.

beats.activity: Prepare and start sourdough dough, make simple dinner; Review project expense spreadsheet, check sourdough fermentation, relax with a book
beats.reasoning: Viktor unwinds by engaging in his sourdough passion; the long daylight and warm weather put him in a relaxed mood. This is his decompression ritual after a full work day.

diary:
Monday started bright and clear—perfect weather, though I barely noticed it stuck in the car heading down to Tukwila. The site was humming; framing's coming along better than schedule, which always feels like a small victory. Grabbed a sandwich at the trailer around one, wolfed it down while checking measurements. The drive back north was smooth, at least—heard on the radio the Light Rail was down, but I'd already bypassed that mess.

Stopped at QFC on the way home and splurged on good flour and fresh starter. Something about a Monday feels right for beginning dough. Made dinner, got the starter going around eight, and spent the evening watching it proof while I fiddled with the expense spreadsheet. Numbers looked decent. Settled into bed with a book, listening to the dough bubble quietly in its jar on the counter. Quiet day, really. The kind that leaves you feeling like you actually moved something forward.
thought_process: I was in a straightforward headspace today—focused on the work itself, not distracted by the World Cup buzz happening downtown. Normally I'd catch a match at a bar, but the Light Rail closure made that logistics annoying, and honestly, I wasn't in the mood. The site going well put me in a good frame of mind, so when I got home I wanted to *make* something rather than consume. Baking grounds me. The spreadsheet work came naturally after that—felt productive without being taxing. The sunny weather helped my mood stay level, though I barely registered it. Sometimes the best days are the quiet ones where everything just slots into place: work runs smooth, commute's easy, and you've got energy left for a hobby. That's what today was.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
